# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:latest

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    nginx \
    python3 \
    python3-pip \
    postgresql-client \
    redis-server \
    net-tools \
    iproute2 \
    procps \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install API server dependencies (example: Flask)
RUN pip3 install flask gunicorn psycopg2-binary redis

# Create directories for services
RUN mkdir -p /app/webserver /app/api /app/database /app/redis

# Example Nginx configuration for the web server
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/sites-available/default
RUN ln -sf /etc/nginx/sites-available/default /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/default
RUN echo "daemon off;" >> /etc/nginx/nginx.conf

# Example API server (Flask app)
COPY app.py /app/api/app.py

# Example Database setup (not a real database server, just client and placeholder)
# For an actual database server in the same container, you'd install postgresql-server
# and configure it to run. This example assumes a separate DB or simple client usage.

# Redis configuration (default is usually fine for dev)
RUN sed -i 's/^bind 127.0.0.1/bind 0.0.0.0/' /etc/redis/redis.conf
RUN sed -i 's/^protected-mode yes/protected-mode no/' /etc/redis/redis.conf

# Create a startup script
COPY start.sh /usr/local/bin/start.sh
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/start.sh

# Expose ports for all services
EXPOSE 80 8080 5432 6379

CMD ["/usr/local/bin/start.sh"]

# --- Additional files to be copied (nginx.conf, app.py, start.sh) ---

# nginx.conf (example)
# server {
#     listen 80;
#     server_name localhost;

#     location / {
#         proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:8080; # Proxy requests to the API server
#         proxy_set_header Host $host;
#         proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
#         proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
#         proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
#     }

#     error_page 500 502 503 504 /50x.html;
#     location = /50x.html {
#         root /usr/share/nginx/html;
#     }
# }

# app.py (example Flask API server)
# from flask import Flask
# import os
# import time
# import redis
# import psycopg2

# app = Flask(__name__)

# # Redis connection
# r = redis.Redis(host='127.0.0.1', port=6379, db=0)

# @app.route('/')
# def hello():
#     count = r.incr('hits')
#     try:
#         # Example: connect to PostgreSQL (assuming it's running locally or available)
#         # For this specific setup, 5432 is exposed but no DB server is truly running inside
#         # This would typically be for a separate DB container or a full local install
#         conn = psycopg2.connect(host="127.0.0.1", port=5432, user="user", password="password", database="mydb", connect_timeout=1)
#         cur = conn.cursor()
#         cur.execute("SELECT version();")
#         db_version = cur.fetchone()[0]
#         cur.close()
#         conn.close()
#         db_status = f"Connected to PostgreSQL: {db_version}"
#     except Exception as e:
#         db_status = f"Could not connect to PostgreSQL: {e}"

#     html = f"<h3>Hello from the API server!</h3>" \
#            f"<b>Hits:</b> {count}<br/>" \
#            f"<b>Database Status:</b> {db_status}<br/>" \
#            f"<b>Hostname:</b> {os.getenv('HOSTNAME')}<br/>" \
#            f"<p>This is a multi-service container.</p>"
#     return html

# if __name__ == '__main__':
#     app.run(debug=True, host='0.0.0.0', port=8080)

# start.sh (example startup script)
# #!/bin/bash

# # Start Redis server
# redis-server /etc/redis/redis.conf &
# echo "Redis server started."

# # Start Nginx web server
# nginx &
# echo "Nginx web server started."

# # Start Gunicorn for the Flask API server
# # Ensure the API server starts last or has a retry mechanism if it depends on others
# # For a full database, you'd start PostgreSQL here as well.
# # Example: pg_ctl -D /var/lib/postgresql/data start
# gunicorn --bind 0.0.0.0:8080 app:app --chdir /app/api &
# echo "API server (Gunicorn) started."

# # Keep the container running
# tail -f /dev/null
